tasks.subject: chat-streaming-session-reliability [P2] Skip relay_context in transcript-reader title extraction
tasks.description:
Fix session titles showing relay metadata like `Agent-ID: ...` instead of the first real user message (P1) by adding a `<relay_context>` skip condition to the `extractSessionMeta()` private method in `apps/server/src/services/session/transcript-reader.ts`.

**Root cause:** The `extractSessionMeta()` method reads the first ~8KB of a JSONL file to extract the session title from the first user message. It already skips `<local-command`, `<command-name>`, `<command-message>`, and `<task-notification>` prefixes. When Relay is enabled, the first user message in the JSONL is the `<relay_context>` block injected by `ClaudeCodeAdapter`, causing all relay session titles to show relay metadata instead of the user's actual question.

**Implementation steps:**

1. Open `apps/server/src/services/session/transcript-reader.ts`.

2. Locate the `extractSessionMeta()` private method. Find the block that skips internal message types (around line 247-254). The current block reads:

```typescript
if (
  text.startsWith('<local-command') ||
  text.startsWith('<command-name>') ||
  text.startsWith('<command-message>') ||
  text.startsWith('<task-notification>')
) {
  continue;
}
```

3. Add `<relay_context>` to this skip condition:

```typescript
if (
  text.startsWith('<local-command') ||
  text.startsWith('<command-name>') ||
  text.startsWith('<command-message>') ||
  text.startsWith('<task-notification>') ||
  text.startsWith('<relay_context>')    // Filter relay metadata injected by ClaudeCodeAdapter
) {
  continue;
}
```

**Effect:** When iterating JSONL lines to find the first user message for the title, relay metadata lines are skipped. The title is derived from the next non-internal user message. For sessions that start with only relay-injected content and no subsequent user message in the head buffer, the title falls back to `Session <uuid-prefix>` as before.

**Scope:** Affects only `extractSessionMeta()` which is used by both `listSessions()` (sidebar session list) and `getSession()` (single session metadata). This fixes both the sidebar titles and the session detail view.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- A session whose first JSONL user message starts with `<relay_context>` gets a title derived from the first non-relay user message.
- A session whose only user message is `<relay_context>` gets the fallback `Session <uuid-prefix>` title.
- Session titles for non-relay sessions (no `<relay_context>` in JSONL) are completely unaffected.
- The added condition is in the same `if` block as the existing skip conditions (not a separate `if` block).
tasks.activeForm: Skipping relay_context in session title extraction

tasks.subject: chat-streaming-session-reliability [P2] Merge PATCH response into TanStack Query cache instead of replacing
tasks.description:
Fix the model/permission mode selector cross-reset bug (P2) by using the TanStack Query updater-function form of `setQueryData` in `apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/use-session-status.ts`.

**Root cause:** The PATCH `/api/sessions/:id` response may not include both `model` and `permissionMode`. When the client updates only `model`, the server reads the session from disk — which may not yet reflect a previously-set `permissionMode` (stored only in agentManager memory, not yet written to JSONL) — and returns a partial object. `queryClient.setQueryData(key, updated)` replaces the entire cache entry, discarding the cached `permissionMode`. The user then sees the permission mode reset to the default.

**Current code** (`use-session-status.ts` line 84):
```typescript
const updated = await transport.updateSession(sessionId, opts, selectedCwd ?? undefined);
queryClient.setQueryData(['session', sessionId, selectedCwd], updated);
```

**Implementation steps:**

1. Open `apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/use-session-status.ts`.

2. In the `updateSession` callback (the `useCallback` starting around line 76), locate the `queryClient.setQueryData` call after `await transport.updateSession(...)`. Change from replace to merge:

```typescript
// Before:
queryClient.setQueryData(['session', sessionId, selectedCwd], updated);

// After:
queryClient.setQueryData(
  ['session', sessionId, selectedCwd],
  (old: Session | undefined) => ({ ...old, ...updated })
);
```

3. Add the `Session` import from `@dorkos/shared/types` if not already imported:

```typescript
import type {
  SessionStatusEvent,
  PermissionMode,
  UpdateSessionRequest,
  Session,
} from '@dorkos/shared/types';
```

**Why this works:** The updater-function form receives the current cache value as `old`. Spreading `{ ...old, ...updated }` preserves all previously-cached fields while applying the server's new values on top. If `old` is `undefined` (cache miss), spreading `undefined` in an object literal is a no-op — safe by JavaScript spec.

**Defense-in-depth note from spec:** The server-side PATCH handler could also be improved to always return both `model` and `permissionMode` from in-memory state when the disk transcript hasn't been updated yet. This is optional for the bug fix (client-side merge alone is sufficient) but improves the API response completeness. The current handler at `routes/sessions.ts` around line 131 already calls `agentManager.updateSession()` and then `transcriptReader.getSession()` — the in-memory `updated` result from `agentManager.updateSession()` has both fields. Consider patching the response to include `model: updated.model` and `permissionMode: updated.permissionMode` as well to ensure completeness, rather than relying solely on the disk-read session object.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- After updating `model`, the TanStack Query cache entry still contains the previously-set `permissionMode`.
- After updating `permissionMode`, the cache entry still contains the previously-set `model`.
- The status bar does not visually reset either field when the other is updated.
- If `transport.updateSession` throws, the optimistic rollback (`setLocalModel(null)` / `setLocalPermissionMode(null)`) still fires correctly — no change needed to the catch block.
tasks.activeForm: Merging PATCH response into TanStack Query cache
